concerns.text: T016 implements a 'Formal Power Analysis' gate that halts the pipeline if power < 0.8. This contradicts spec.md FR-001 and Assumptions, which mandate that if N < 100 (implying low power), the system MUST 'flag the study as exploratory' or 'attempt to supplement with TCGA', not halt execution. The task silently weakens the spec's contingency plan into a hard stop.

T012 is marked [X] but includes a comment '<!-- FAILED: unspecified -->'. FR-001 requires downloading and parsing GTEx v8 data. A task marked as failed/unspecified cannot satisfy the requirement to 'attempt to classify every sample' or provide the data for subsequent gates. This is a semantic weakening of the deliverable.

T015b is marked [X] but includes a comment '<!-- FAILED: unspecified -->'. FR-002 and Constitution Principle VI require strict filtering to the core circadian gene list. A failed task cannot satisfy the requirement to filter the matrix, leaving the core hypothesis testing (US-02) without the mandated input data.

T027 is marked [X] but includes a comment '<!-- SKIPPED: YAML+regex parse failed... -->'. FR-007 mandates that the correlation method MUST be Spearman by default, switching to Pearson ONLY if normality is confirmed (Shapiro-Wilk). A skipped normality check means the system cannot enforce this conditional logic, violating the statistical correction requirement.

SC-005 requires measuring sensitivity against ATP-III threshold variations. T042 (Sensitivity Analysis) and T017 (Baseline Labels) are marked [ ] (unimplemented). Without these tasks, the spec's mandatory success criterion (SC-005) has no implementation path, effectively dropping the acceptance criterion.

FR-008 requires generating diagnostic plots (heatmap, ROC curve) and US-03 requires collinearity diagnostics (VIF). Tasks T036 (Collinearity), T037 (ROC), and T038 (Heatmap) are marked [ ] (unimplemented). This drops the acceptance criteria for model validation and visualization required by the spec.
